Title: HY5 functions as a systemic signal by integrating BRC1-dependent hormone signaling in tomato bud outgrowth
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Integrates BR signaling in bud outgrowth control
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active BZR1 promotes bud outgrowth by suppressing BRC1; reduced BZR1 accumulation under low R/FR correlates with bud inhibition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; western blot of dBZR1; light treatment comparisons
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein abundance in buds decreases under low R/FR; limited diurnal change
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Accumulated in lateral buds; active dephosphorylated form associated with outgrowth
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red ratio; sucrose application; diurnal cycle
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important mechanistic component, but not the main focal gene in this study's nutrient-metabolism-like hormonal causality extraction relative to HY5/BRC1 module
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light quality influences BZR1 accumulation in buds. 2. BZR1 contributes to BR-dependent repression of BRC1. 3. Bud outgrowth control also includes BZR1-independent light pathways.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for architecture engineering through BR signaling modulation, though broad pleiotropy is expected.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native; also promoter analyzed in dual-luciferase and ChIP/EMSA assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced steroid intermediates leading toward brassinolide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a steroid 5alpha-reduction step in brassinosteroid biosynthesis contributing to brassinolide production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud outgrowth and release of apical dominance in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DET2 expression is associated with increased BL accumulation and promoted bud outgrowth; reduced expression under low R/FR or HY5 deficiency correlates with suppressed bud outgrowth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under light treatments and HY5 transgenic backgrounds; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in lateral buds; reduced under low R/FR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds; induced by HY5 and reduced by low R/FR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red light ratio; HY5 overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a direct HY5 target linking light signaling to BR biosynthesis in buds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DET2 transcript decreases under low R/FR conditions in lateral buds. 2. HY5 directly binds the DET2 promoter and activates its expression. 3. Increased DET2 expression correlates with higher BL and enhanced bud outgrowth.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering branching architecture via modulation of local BR biosynthesis, but whole-plant BR pleiotropy should be considered.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native; promoter analyzed in dual-luciferase and ChIP/EMSA assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: BR biosynthetic intermediates contributing to BL formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in brassinosteroid biosynthesis to support brassinolide accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud outgrowth and release of apical dominance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DWF expression is associated with increased BL accumulation and promoted bud outgrowth; lower expression under low R/FR or HY5 deficiency correlates with bud suppression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dwf mutant phenotyping; qRT-PCR; hormone measurement; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in lateral buds; reduced under low R/FR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red light ratio; HY5 overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The dwf mutant was used to show BR-dependent and BR-independent components of light-regulated bud outgrowth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DWF transcript is reduced by low R/FR in lateral buds. 2. HY5 directly binds and activates the DWF promoter. 3. DWF contributes to local BL accumulation associated with bud outgrowth.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning shoot branching through BR biosynthesis, with caution about broad BR effects on development.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: BRC1 promoter, tissue-focused expression in buds (pBRC1-LOG4)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Local cytokinin activation in lateral buds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts cytokinin nucleotide precursors into active cytokinin bases, thereby increasing local cytokinin levels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote lateral bud outgrowth and branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LOG4 promotes CK accumulation and bud outgrowth; BRC1 represses LOG4 to inhibit branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; pBRC1-LOG4 transgenics; VIGS silencing; CK quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In lateral buds; upregulated in brc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds, under control of BRC1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light growth conditions; BRC1 mutation or local transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of lateral buds from WT, brc1 #5, brc1 #9; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific expression from the BRC1 promoter in buds was sufficient to promote branching
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOG4 transcript is strongly increased in brc1 buds. 2. BRC1 directly binds the LOG4 promoter and represses its activity. 3. Bud-specific LOG4 expression promotes branching, while LOG4 silencing reduces branching.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive branching when overexpressed in buds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for local engineering of branch number through bud-specific cytokinin activation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: BRC1 promoter, tissue-focused expression in buds (pBRC1-CKX7)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Local cytokinin catabolism in lateral buds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative degradation of cytokinins, reducing local active cytokinin levels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote lateral bud outgrowth and branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CKX7 reduces cytokinin accumulation and suppresses bud outgrowth; BRC1 activates CKX7 to inhibit branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; pBRC1-CKX7 transgenics; VIGS silencing; CK quantification in brc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In lateral buds; downregulated in brc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds, positively regulated by BRC1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light growth conditions; BRC1 mutation or bud-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of lateral buds from WT, brc1 #5, brc1 #9; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bud-specific CKX7 accumulation significantly reduced branching
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CKX7 transcript decreases markedly in brc1 mutants. 2. BRC1 directly binds and activates the CKX7 promoter. 3. Bud-specific CKX7 expression suppresses branching, whereas CKX7 silencing promotes branching.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced branching when overexpressed in buds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for reducing unwanted branching via local cytokinin degradation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native; attempted pBRC1-driven overexpression failed to regenerate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation of gibberellins, reducing active GA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gibberellins (GA1, GA3, GA4 and related pool)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote lateral bud outgrowth in tomato buds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GA2ox4 suppresses GA accumulation and bud outgrowth; BRC1 activates GA2ox4 to inhibit branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; VIGS cosilencing with GA2ox5; GA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In lateral buds; decreased in brc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds, positively regulated by BRC1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light growth conditions; BRC1 mutation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of lateral buds from WT, brc1 #5, brc1 #9; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pBRC1-GA2ox4 plants could not be recovered, likely due to low GA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2ox4 transcript is reduced in brc1 mutants. 2. BRC1 directly binds and activates the GA2ox4 promoter. 3. Cosilencing GA2ox4 and GA2ox5 promotes branching, supporting their inhibitory role.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ectopic expression may impair regeneration or growth by excessive GA depletion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for suppressing branching, but strong expression may cause severe GA-related growth defects.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native; attempted pBRC1-driven overexpression failed to regenerate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation of gibberellins, reducing active GA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gibberellins (GA1, GA3, GA4 and related pool)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote lateral bud outgrowth in tomato buds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GA2ox5 suppresses GA accumulation and bud outgrowth; BRC1 activates GA2ox5 to inhibit branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; VIGS cosilencing with GA2ox4; GA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In lateral buds; decreased in brc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds, positively regulated by BRC1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light growth conditions; BRC1 mutation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of lateral buds from WT, brc1 #5, brc1 #9; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts together with GA2ox4 downstream of BRC1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2ox5 transcript is reduced in brc1 mutants. 2. BRC1 directly binds and activates the GA2ox5 promoter. 3. Cosilencing GA2ox4 and GA2ox5 promotes branching, supporting their role in repressing bud outgrowth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ectopic expression may impair growth due to GA depletion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-branching target, but deployment requires careful spatial control.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S or overexpression promoter not specified for HY5-OE; native promoter context also analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BL, CK, and GA promote bud outgrowth and branching in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 promotes bud outgrowth by repressing BRC1 and activating BR biosynthesis genes, thereby favoring BL accumulation and indirectly enabling higher CK and GA status
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; RNAi; overexpression; grafting; bud shading; qRT-PCR; western blot; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of BRC1 and direct transcriptional activation of DET2 and DWF; systemic shoot-to-bud mobility
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses BRC1; activates DET2 and DWF
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Bud-local BR biosynthesis and BRC1-centered hormonal control of branching
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major light-induced positive regulator and systemic signal promoting bud outgrowth
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light quality; PHYB1B2; PHYA; CRY1a; sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; also mobile between organs as protein/signal
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher HY5 transcript/protein in buds during day; lower under low R/FR and in phyB1B2 and cry1a mutants; higher in phyA and CRY1a-OE
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves and lateral buds; accumulates during day and moves from leaves to buds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red ratio; white light; diurnal cycle; sucrose application; grafting; bud shading
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a mobile systemic signal from leaves to lateral buds; shading buds had little effect, supporting leaf-derived HY5 contribution
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is required for light-induced bud outgrowth in tomato. 2. HY5 directly suppresses BRC1 transcription and directly activates DET2 and DWF. 3. HY5 protein acts systemically from leaves to buds and contributes to diurnal bud growth regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on photomorphogenesis and other light-regulated processes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for architectural engineering through light-responsive branching control, but broad HY5 functions require tissue- or context-specific strategies.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: BRC1 promoter used for local expression of LOG4 and CKX7
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: CK and GA promote bud outgrowth and branching
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BRC1 suppresses bud outgrowth by reducing CK and GA accumulation in lateral buds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutants; double mutant analysis with hy5; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase; hormone profiling; VIGS; bud-specific transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of LOG4 and activation of CKX7, GA2ox4, and GA2ox5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses LOG4; activates CKX7, GA2ox4, and GA2ox5
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: CK biosynthesis/activation, CK degradation, and GA degradation in lateral buds
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central integrator and key repressor of branching by controlling local CK and GA status
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in lateral buds under low R/FR and at night; reduced by HY5 and in brc1 mutants absent/disrupted
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral buds, especially associated with dormant or growth-suppressed buds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red ratio; diurnal cycle; HY5 genetic background; white light
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of lateral buds from WT, brc1 #5, brc1 #9; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5; BZR1; LOG4 promoter; CKX7 promoter; GA2ox4 promoter; GA2ox5 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: brc1 mutation rescued the hy5 branching defect, placing BRC1 downstream of HY5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BRC1 acts downstream of HY5 to repress bud outgrowth. 2. BRC1 directly lowers CK by repressing LOG4 and increasing CKX7. 3. BRC1 directly lowers GA by activating GA2ox4 and GA2ox5.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of BRC1 causes excessive branching
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for modifying branch architecture, especially where reduced apical dominance is desired.
